title: Canvas Items, Tags, Layers, Rendering, and Redraw · Program Brief: Canvas Item Model
summary: Redraw duplicates thousands of line items, solver marks cover walls, stale IDs are mutated, and hit testing scans every object. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the canvas item model increment in tagged layered Canvas render plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.
Reconcile cell count, neighbor pairs, reciprocal openings, boundary exceptions, and illegal diagonal links.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-canvas-item-model — Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-canvas-rendering-tags-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-canvas-rendering-tags-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_canvas_rendering_tags_4(expected_items: int = 126, actual_items: int = 126, semantic_tags: int = 5, duplicate_items: int = 0, layer_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0:
        return False, actual_items, "render-count"
    if actual_items != expected_items:
        return False, actual_items, "item-reconciliation"
    if semantic_tags < 4:
        return False, semantic_tags, "tag-contract"
    if duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0:
        return False, duplicate_items + layer_errors, "render-invariant"
    assert actual_items == expected_items
    return True, actual_items, "tagged-render-plan"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Canvas Item Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 7358 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Canvas Item Model model · Theory Canvas Item Model.
steps.why: Retrieving Canvas Item Model through the concrete tagged layered Canvas render plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Canvas Item Model model · Theory Canvas Item Model, keep Canvas Item Model connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Canvas Item Model model · Theory Canvas Item Model, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Canvas Item Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Canvas Item Model model · Theory Canvas Item Model, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 7358 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Canvas Item Model model · Theory Canvas Item Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Canvas draw calls disappear after presentation like immediate-mode graphics.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-canvas-item-model-1lmrcd(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_canvas_item_model_1lmrcd\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?actual_items != expected_items)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?semantic_tags < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
